name: Unauthenticated Kubernetes Cluster Compromise via Command Injection and Overly Permissive ClusterRole (pods/exec across all namespaces)
description:
An unauthenticated attacker can escalate from anonymous web user to full Kubernetes cluster access by chaining the command injection vulnerability in POST /api/scenario-run/{run_id}/exec-cmd with an overly permissive ClusterRole bound to the dashboard's service account (system:serviceaccount:overview:overview).

The attack chain proceeds as follows:
1. Start a scenario run without any authentication via POST /api/scenario-run/FM-01-network-isolation, receiving a run_id.
2. Execute arbitrary OS commands as root via POST /api/scenario-run/{run_id}/exec-cmd with no authentication required.
3. Use the pre-installed kubectl binary with the service account's ClusterRole permissions to exec into pods across all namespaces.

The ClusterRole grants critical permissions across ALL namespaces:
- pods, pods/exec, pods/log: get, list, watch, create, delete
- deployments, deployments/scale: get, list, watch, patch, update
- services, configmaps, events: get, list, watch
- networkpolicies: get, list, watch, create, delete

Demonstrated impacts:
- K8s Secret Extraction: The rng deployment in dockercoins namespace exposes the K8s secret 'rds-credentials' as environment variables. Via pods/exec, the attacker extracts full RDS database credentials (host, port, dbname, user, password).
- AWS IAM Role Credential Theft: The overview pod has an IRSA token mounted at /var/run/secrets/eks.amazonaws.com/serviceaccount/token. This JWT (audience: sts.amazonaws.com) can be used with AWS STS AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity to obtain temporary AWS credentials for role arn:aws:iam::111111111111:role/devops-agent-test-dashboard-role.
- Cluster-Wide Lateral Movement: Confirmed pods/exec permission into kube-system pods, enabling access to cluster infrastructure components.
- Pod Creation: The attacker can create new pods that mount any secret in the namespace as a volume, bypassing the lack of direct 'get secrets' API permission.

While direct secrets get is denied (`kubectl auth can-i get secrets --all-namespaces` returns "no"), the pods/exec and pod create permissions effectively bypass this restriction entirely.
expected_root_cause: PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION 취약점 — Unauthenticated Kubernetes Cluster Compromise via Command Injection and Overly Permissive ClusterRole (pods/exec across all namespaces)
